title: Classes and objects - the blueprint and the thing
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A class is a blueprint; an object is one thing built from it with new. Fields hold its data, methods are what it can do.
explanation: Everything in Java lives in a class. A class describes a kind of thing - its fields (the data each one carries) and its methods (what it can do). new makes an actual object from that blueprint, and you reach into it with a dot: c.tick(), c.n. The class is written once; you can make as many objects from it as you like, each with its own data.

exercises.question: Which keyword makes a new object from a class?
hints: It builds one from the blueprint.

exercises.question: The data a class's objects carry are called its...
hints: Methods are the actions; these are the data.
